item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game’s producer.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: We got an early look at three of the biggest games coming to Switch 2. This is our verdict
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Man in intensive care after brawl at Melbourne pizza shop
item.description: A Melbourne man is in intensive care after a brawl broke out at a pizza shop in front of shocked diners.

item.title: At least 23,000 jobs in tech have been lost to AI this year. Experts say worse is still to come
item.description: T﻿ech company bosses have had similar things to say about AI.

item.title: Atlassian accused of illegally firing employee who suggested boss was 'rich jerk'
item.description: P﻿rosecutors argued that the employee was merely acting in the company's "Open Company, No Bullshit" philosophy.

item.title: Iconic Stonewall Hotel goes under after 28 years as administrators appointed
item.description: The owner said it was a "sad and difficult" decision to appoint administrators. ﻿

item.title: 'I don't accept it': Kyle Sandilands reveals contract terminated by ARN
item.description: T﻿he shock jock said ARN "decided to try and burn the place down" over an on-air spat with Jackie O.

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies aged 43
item.description: Radvinsky transformed the British-based company into an online pornography powerhouse.

item.title: Why we can continue to call our cheese feta, but fontina will be a no-go
item.description: T﻿he names used in dozens of common goods will change under new rules in the recently inked Australia-European Union trade agreement.

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.title: Businesses urge government to decrease $3 billion tax to avoid 'even worse outcomes' in fuel crisis
item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: Apple almost went broke – now it's celebrating 50 years
item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.title: 'Doesn't pass the pub test': Hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges
item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.title: 'It has been a privilege': Cusack's Furniture to close late 2026
item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.title: New COVID strain circulating in Australia as it spreads across the globe
item.description: Experts say there are no signs the variant BA.3.2, otherwise known as "Cicada", poses a bigger health threat than other variations.

item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.description: A﻿ustralian tech giant Canva has snapped up two local artificial intelligence (AI) companies. But bigger news is on the way.

item.description: T﻿heir bottom line will be impacted by any prolonged disruption of aviation fuel, analysts warn.

item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.description: Alarm bells went off in small business owner Tanielle's mind as soon as she saw it.

item.description: Payslip complacency has reached remarkable highs.

item.title: Virgin to cut flights, raise costs after $40 million fuel hit
item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.description: A﻿ nation-wide study of our wastewater and online search data has revealed the worrying trend is booming.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after 15 years
item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.title: What does the National Disability Insurance Scheme overhaul mean for you?
item.description: The government is set to slash the cost of running the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and aged care under sweeping new changes.

item.description: T﻿he inquiry has been marked by scuffles between investigators and delivery service employees, a feigned medical emergency and hastily scribbled notes to "stay silent".

item.description: They seem harmless upon first glance, but look a little closer and you'll see the truth.

item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.description: People used to shame it, but clearly something is working for Aussie shoppers.

item.description: Anyone who has been to these locations recently should monitor for symptoms, authorities say.

item.title: Woolworths sales surge but supermarket cuts profit forecast amid petrol price spike
item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.description: R﻿esidents of Philip Island in Victoria are bracing for a dual battle to stop the stores from opening.

item.title: Bleak rate hike assessment: 'Australians are poorer' and 'everything is moving in the wrong direction'
item.description: The Reserve Bank's third successive rate hike hit mortgage holders like a kick in the guts and there is little light to be found at the end of the tunnel when digging deeper.

item.description: It's the worst remuneration strike in recent Australian corporate history in a stunning blow to the embattled network.

item.description: The carpark construction, which would have created an additional 296 parking spots, would have come at the expense of the Hyper Karting track, axing more than 150 jobs in the process.

item.title: Small businesses and tradies to permanently keep $20,000 tax break in federal budget
item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.description: "I just feel that there was no future for our generations to come."

item.description: Katy Gallagher has been criticised for saying children are better off the earlier they enter early education or childcare.

item.title: Net migration figures to be 35,000 higher than expected
item.description: Immigration surged following the pandemic as the influx caught up from years of border restrictions, peaking at more than 550,000 in a year.

item.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer defiant as calls for his resignation grow and several ministers quit
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has insisted he has no intention of resigning as calls grew louder within his Labour Party for him to step down and some junior members of his government quit in protest.

item.description: Retired NSW Police Commissioner Karen Webb will jointly lead a review into the Northern Territory's child protection system sparking by the alleged murder of five-year-old Alice Springs girl, Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Why it could take years for first buyers to benefit from housing tax reforms
item.description: While the changes were touted as the most significant of the 21st century, it may take years for ﻿lower prices and easier access to the market to reach aspiring first-home buyers. ﻿

item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa has stayed under the protection of allied senators as Philippine authorities tried to arrest him for a charge of crime against humanity.

item.description: On a day when the British government's legislative plans were presented by no less than King Charles III himself, Prime Minister Keir Starmer was fighting to remain in power.

item.description: Timmy, the young humpback whale who was stranded for weeks in shallow waters, has been found dead just off Denmark's coast.

item.description: The wife of one of Hollywood's biggest stars died in a freak accident during a game of hide-and-seek on May 19, 1946.

item.description: Anthony Albanese said "what does a royal commission do besides fund lawyers?"

item.description: The fashion magnate was hiking with his son when he fell about 150 metres down a cliff and died. Seventeen months later his son was arrested.

item.description: Last week's changes to the ﻿capital gains tax discount and negative gearing will likely shift people away from rentvesting.

item.description: The English town of Staines changed its name on May 20, 2012, in an effort to distance itself from a non-existent person.

item.description: I﻿t is the first British police investigation linked to Jeffrey Epstein over alleged sexual abuse.

item.description: She was last heard repeatedly screaming "I’m dying, I’m dying".

item.description: An unarmed British Royal Air Force reconnaissance plane was subjected to a series of "dangerous and unacceptable" intercepts by Russian fighter jets in international airspace over the Black Sea, UK's Ministry of Defence said.

item.description: T﻿here have been more than 230 cases in most states and territories.

item.title: Teen allegedly made to-do list before hijacking attempt

item.title: Man says he was 'accused of being a terrorist' after laser hair removal device shut down Victorian airport
item.description: The man told 9News he was rushing to catch his plane when one of his bags triggered an evacuation inside the domestic terminal.

item.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car in Cairns
item.description: A police car in pursuit of a wanted person has struck and killed a mother while she was crossing the road in Cairns.

item.title: Driver fighting for life, kids hospitalised after bus crashes into tree in Sydney
item.description: A year 12 student has grabbed the wheel of a bus in an attempt to steer it to safety after the driver suffered a medical episode.

item.description: Shocked witnesses tell of a big "glowing ball ... falling from the sky" like they had never seen before.

item.title: Queensland Olympics minister quits cabinet position after AFP referral
item.description: The man in charge of bringing Brisbane 2032 to life is facing the prospect of a criminal investigation into whether he committed electoral fraud.

item.title: Family's battle to save Adelaide man found with mystery brain injury in Indonesia
item.description: The family of a young Adelaide man say they are desperately trying to get him home to Australia after he was found unconscious on a remote road in Indonesia.

item.title: Today in History - May 22: Dictator hated movie so much, he took 200 people hostage
item.description: General Idi Amin Dada: A Self-Portrait premiered at Cannes Film Festival on May 22, 1974.

item.title: Islamophobic TikToker made call for another Cronulla riot
item.description: A﻿ court has decided the 20-year-old man who threatened violence against Middle Eastern people and Muslims will leave prison.

item.title: Documents show Queen Elizabeth was eager for ex-prince Andrew to become trade envoy
item.description: T﻿he Queen was "very keen" to see her son take on a "prominent role", newly released documents have revealed.

item.title: Snow, high winds and rain heading to multiple states in early taste of winter
item.description: M﻿illions of people will be pulling on their woollies and reaching for umbrellas as a big weather change takes hold.

item.title: 'Not good timing for me': Trump may skip son's wedding
item.description: H﻿is eldest son is due to be wed on a private island in the Bahamas, but the US president may not attend.

item.title: Plane forced to divert over Ebola fears after Congolese passenger boarded 'in error'
item.description: A customs spokesperson said the passenger "should not have boarded" at all.

item.title: 'It will be hard': Parents warned as childcare workers vote in favour of mass industrial action
item.description: Thousands of childcare workers around the country could walk off the job on July 15 after union members voted in favour of mass industrial action.

item.title: Opposition leader backs Jacinta Price after questions about migration remark
item.description: Jacinta Price denied agreeing to a remark that Australia needs to stop "flooding" itself with migrants from India, China, Africa and the Middle East.

item.title: Government 'unaware' of booked flights for ISIS brides returning home
item.description: Six women, their children and grandchildren have reportedly left a Syrian camp for Australia.

item.title: Teacher charged with grooming former student, possessing abuse material
item.description: T﻿he 35-year-old man was arrested during a police raid on a property in south-west Sydney.

item.title: Sydney council to charge fees for hosting pop-up picnics, weddings and gender reveal parties in popular parks
item.description: Many of North Sydney Council's popular parks and reserves boast picturesque views of the sparkling Sydney Harbour – but using them for events could soon some at a high price.

item.title: Childcare workers who assaulted toddler avoid conviction
item.description: Two childcare workers who pleaded guilty to assaulting a misbehaving toddler have avoided conviction.

item.title: Guzman y Gomez scuttles plans for US expansion after weak sales, immediately scraps eight stores
item.description: G﻿uzman y Gomez has scuttled its plans for an ambitious expansion in the United States and will immediately shut its existing stores in Chicago.

item.title: Barnaby Joyce explains moment Pauline Hanson's aide tells reporter to 'shut up'
item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader made the remark just seconds after her adviser said 'shut up' to a journalist.

item.title: Man accused of sexually assaulting elderly woman still on the run in NT
item.description: Authorities have spent days canvassing the area for CCTV footage and are continuing to appeal for information.

item.title: Trump's giant arch takes big step forward
item.description: T﻿he president has issued a grim warning for members of a small nation that has long been a thorn in the si...

item.title: One-punch killer Kieran Loveridge escapes further monitoring out jail
item.description: While Kieran Loveridge will not be subject to extra monitoring at the end of his sentence for a one-punch killing, an attempt to suppress his identity failed.

item.title: Everest sees record 274 climbers in single day, including Aussie milestone
item.description: Mountaineers rushed to take advantage of a rare stretch of clear weather before conditions deteriorate.

item.title: Major drugs haul found hidden inside machinery at Australian port
item.description: P﻿olice experts spent three days taking the equipment apart before uncovering 370kg of illicit substances.

item.title: Police issue fresh appeal for witnesses as investigation into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor continues
item.description: The update comes about three months after the former prince﻿ was arrested.

item.title: PM refuses to rule out changes to the 'death tax'
item.description: Labor has been fielding questions about the so-called death tax during its nationwide campaign to promote the budget, which it has dismissed as a Coalition scare campaign.

item.title: Truck hits dozens of sprinklers in busy Sydney tunnel
item.description: Footage shows the moment the system activated after being bumped by the truck, sending water gushing onto the road below the airport tarmac.

item.title: Trio charged with murder over man's Sydney balcony fall death
item.description: One neighbour said he woke in the early hours of the morning to desperate cries for help.

item.title: Manhunt underway in Vaucluse after cars smashed, house broken
item.description: A man reportedly broke into a house and stole a car before smashing into multiple others.

item.title: Victorian police officer dies after falling during hike on Inca Trail in Peru
item.description: Matt Paton has been remembered by his colleagues as someone who loved "travelling, exploring historic sites and cultures".

item.title: Fast food arrest of man over alleged threat of Melbourne CBD incident
item.description: A man has been arrested in Melbourne after an alleged threat of "an incident taking place" on busy Bourke Street tonight.

item.title: Tribute to woman hit and killed by police vehicle in Cairns
item.description: A friend of a Cairns mother who was hit and killed by a police vehicle has shared their heartbreak.

item.title: 'So loved by so many': Tributes to dad killed in shark attack off Rottnest Island
item.description: With feet in the sand at one of his favourite beaches, loved ones remembered the dad who would want his girls to carry on his love of the ocean.

item.title: US says 'slight progress' in Iran talks amid uncertainty about whether war will resume
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says there was "slight progress" during talks with Iran amid uncertainty about whether a deal will be reached or war will resume.

item.title: Today in History - May 23: Child actor had no idea he was starring in a horror movie
item.description: The classic horror film The Shining, starring Jack Nicholson and directed by Stanley Kubrick, was released to American cinemas on May 23, 1980.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as director of national intelligence, citing her husband's health
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard has resigned as President Donald Trump's director of national intelligence.

item.title: Donald Trump confirms he won't attend his son's wedding, claims to be too busy
item.description: Donald Trump has announced he won't attend his son's wedding, saying he is too busy.

item.title: Pentagon releases second batch of declassified UFO files following previous release of more than 160 files
item.description: P﻿olls have found that most US citizens believe aliens exist, and the latest batch of files from the Pentagon has done little to dispel the theories.

item.title: Police appeal over missing five-month-old baby in Sydney
item.description: A five-month old baby boy has gone missing in Sydney's west.

item.title: Trump posts AI video of himself attacking Stephen Colbert
item.description: Donald Trump has posted AI-generated video of himself throwing Stephen Colbert into a dumpster.

item.title: Congo curtails funeral wakes in Ebola outbreak as WHO upgrades risk assessment
item.description: Authorities in north eastern Congo banned funeral wakes and gatherings of more than 50 people on Friday.

item.title: 'Good man' and rideshare driver killed in triple fatal Sydney crash
item.description: Seamus Duignan was on his way home from his 28th birthday celebrations when "horror" struck.

item.title: Woman and toddler killed in horror crash in Queensland's north
item.description: T﻿heir passenger was taken to hospital with life-threatening injuries.

item.title: One Nation would form federal opposition, Labor re-elected, according to new polling
item.description: Federal Labor would be returned to power, the Coalition wiped from the map in four states and One Nation the official opposition party.

item.title: New footage emerges after Sydney underworld figure shot dead in Vietnam
item.description: Coconut Cartel member Lorenzo Laemalu, 24, was shot dead outside a restaurant in Ho Chi Minh.

item.title: Snake catcher's heartbreak after python eats beloved family pet
item.description: A﻿ snake catcher has issued a warning to pet owners after a heartbreaking call-out in Queensland.

item.title: At least 90 killed in China coal mine gas explosion, dozens trapped
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people, state media reported Saturday and after dozens were said to be trapped underground.

item.title: Fears leaking chemical tank will explode, 40,000 told to evacuate in Southern California
item.description: "This thing is going to fail, and we don't know when."
